response.justification: Aisha brings genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, and has demonstrated systems-level thinking via low-latency DLang work at Dunnhumby. However, the role's core requirement is deep, commercial-grade C++ (or Rust) for a performance-critical routing tile pipeline, and the candidate's C++ exposure is minimal — a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project. The primary technology stack throughout the career is JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js. There is no evidence of experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, traffic signal integration, or the kind of high-throughput geospatial data transformation the role demands.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS application, directly relevant to the industry
Demonstrated ability to build distributed, low-latency systems handling millions of requests per second (Dunnhumby media)
Broad systems breadth — Linux, Docker, embedded/hardware work, async I/O, and multi-language experience signal adaptability
concerns: C++ experience is superficial and dated: ~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project; no commercial C++ in routing or geospatial contexts
No Rust experience; no indication of willingness or trajectory toward adopting either required language at depth
Geospatial work centers on data collection and visualization platforms, not routing tile generation, pipeline latency optimization, or navigation guidance systems
